2604.02330.title: ActionParty: Multi-Subject Action Binding in Generative Video Games
2604.02330.paper_summary: Recent advances in video diffusion have enabled the development of "world models" capable of simulating interactive environments. However, these models are largely restricted to single-agent settings, failing to control multiple agents simultaneously in a scene. In this work, we tackle a fundamental issue of action binding in existing video diffusion models, which struggle to associate specific actions with their corresponding subjects. For this purpose, we propose ActionParty, an action controllable multi-subject world model for generative video games. It introduces subject state tokens, i.e. latent variables that persistently capture the state of each subject in the scene. By jointly modeling state tokens and video latents with a spatial biasing mechanism, we disentangle global video frame rendering from individual action-controlled subject updates. We evaluate ActionParty on the Melting Pot benchmark, demonstrating the first video world model capable of controlling up to seven players simultaneously across 46 diverse environments. Our results show significant improvements in action-following accuracy and identity consistency, while enabling robust autoregressive tracking of subjects through complex interactions.
2604.02330.paper_summary_zh: 最近在視頻擴散方面的進展使得能夠開發出能夠模擬互動環境的「世界模型」。<lb/>然而，這些模型在很大程度上僅限於單一代理的設置，無法在場景中同時控制多個代理。<lb/>在這項工作中，我們解決了現有視頻擴散模型中動作綁定的基本問題，這些模型難以將特定動作與其相應的主體關聯起來。<lb/>為此，我們提出了ActionParty，一種可控動作的多主體世界模型，用於生成視頻遊戲。<lb/>它引入了主體狀態標記，即持續捕捉場景中每個主體狀態的潛在變量。<lb/>通過結合狀態標記和視頻潛變量，並使用空間偏置機制，我們將全局視頻幀渲染與個別動作控制的主體更新進行了區分。<lb/>我們在Melting Pot基準上評估了ActionParty，展示了第一個能夠在46個多樣化環境中同時控制多達七名玩家的視頻世界模型。<lb/>我們的結果顯示在動作跟隨準確性和身份一致性方面有顯著改善，同時能夠通過複雜的互動實現穩健的自回歸主體跟踪。
2604.02330.author: Alexander Pondaven et.al.
2604.02330.authors: Alexander Pondaven, Ziyi Wu, Igor Gilitschenski, Philip Torr, Sergey Tulyakov, Fabio Pizzati, Aliaksandr Siarohin
